title: 10 best herbal decoctions for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for jet lag are concentrated liquid extracts made from a combination of herbs, often used to alleviate the symptoms of jet lag. These decoctions help by regulating sleep patterns, reducing stress and fatigue, and improving digestion, all of which are disrupted during long-distance travel. Examples include peppermint, ginger, and valerian root decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or added to baths for relaxation. By incorporating these herbal remedies into their routine, travelers can improve the quality of their sleep, reduce jet lag symptoms, and enjoy a smoother transition to new time zones.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and reduces stress. When our bodies are subjected to sudden time changes, it can disrupt our natural rhythms and make us feel tired, anxious, or irritable. Valerian's calming properties help regulate the body's internal clock by promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, allowing us to adjust to new sleep schedules more easily. As a result, valerian decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia, fatigue, and restlessness, making it an effective natural remedy for travelers.
remedy_properties: Sedative and hypnotic effects: Valerian's sedative and hypnotic properties can help individuals fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, which is essential for adjusting to new time zones.

Adaptogenic and relaxing effects: Valerian's adaptogenic and relaxing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling, allowing the body to adjust to the new environment and schedule more easily.

Calming effect on the nervous system: Valerian's calming effects on the nervous system can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality by reducing symptoms of insomnia and restlessness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and alkaloids, which help regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of valepotriates, compounds that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety associated with travel across time zones.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root in a small bowl.
Boil one cup of water in a medium saucepan for five minutes.
Add the measured valeriana officinalis root to the boiling water and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass cup.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before drinking it all.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with jet lag because its unique blend of flavonoids, terpenes, and alkaloids works to regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms of fatigue. The decoction's calming effects can help ease anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, maypop's ability to stimulate digestion and improve sleep quality ensures that you'll feel rested and refreshed upon arrival at your destination.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Maypop decoction helps regulate the body's response to stress, including travel-related stress, by promoting balance in the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, reducing symptoms of fatigue and anxiety.

Sedative and Calming Effects: The sedative compounds present in maypop decoctions, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and improved sleep quality, which is crucial for recovering from jet lag.

Antioxidant Properties: Maypop decoction's antioxidant properties can help mitigate oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can contribute to fatigue, inflammation, and other symptoms associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in adaptogenic properties, the roots help the body adjust to new time zones and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: Containing natural sedatives, the barks calm the mind and body, promoting a restful sleep and reducing fatigue associated with travel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower flowers and leaves in amounts of 2-3 tablespoons and store them in airtight containers.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of the dried herb and combine it with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Add honey to taste if desired, as well as any other adaptogenic herbs like gaba or valerian root for enhanced relaxation properties.
Consume the passiflora incarnata decoction 30-60 minutes before bedtime and again upon waking up to help regulate sleep-wake cycles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with jet lag because its soothing properties promote relaxation and calmness, easing the body's transition across time zones. The natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender work to reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. As a gentle sedative, it can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of rest, allowing the body to adjust to its new environment more efficiently. This natural remedy supports a healthier adaptation to jet lag, leaving travelers feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender's sedative properties can help regulate sleep-wake cycles, reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, allowing for a more restful night's sleep.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's antioxidants can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can be exacerbated by long flights and time zone changes, while its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe any discomfort or fatigue.

Relaxing and Soothing Effects on the Nervous System: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help ease stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of jet lag such as irritability, headaches, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogens, lavender roots help regulate the body's internal clock and promote relaxation during travel.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, lavender buds calm anxiety and stress related to time zone changes, promoting a restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 250 milliliters of boiling water for infusion.
Steep the lavender flowers in the hot water for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids.
Combine the infused liquid with one teaspoon of honey and stir until dissolved, adjusting sweetness to taste.
Store the decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with jet lag because its soothing properties can calm the mind and body, allowing for a smoother transition across time zones. The apigenin content in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. This, in turn, helps regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms, making it easier to adjust to new sleep patterns and wake-up times. As a result, chamomile decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia, fatigue, and disorientation, allowing travelers to feel more refreshed and revitalized upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions, promoting relaxation and reducing the body's natural response to jet lag.

Sedative properties: Chamomile's sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep, which is crucial for adjusting to a new time zone and combating jet lag symptoms like fatigue and insomnia.

Adaptogenic properties: Chamomile's adaptogenic effects can help the body adapt to changes in its environment, including changes in time zones, reducing stress and promoting resilience against disruptions caused by travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make a calming and sedative decoction that helps regulate sleep patterns and reduces anxiety caused by jet lag.
Flowers: The flowers are a popular choice for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help ease symptoms of fatigue, stress, and insomnia associated with jet travel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning for optimal potency and dry them completely overnight.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers per cup of water and place it in a heat-resistant teapot.
Pour boiling water over the dried flowers and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to release the bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the cooled matricaria chamomilla decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and drink 1 cup three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The decoction contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to balance cortisol levels and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to stimulate the production of melatonin, a hormone responsible for regulating sleep-wake cycles. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing jet lag symptoms, herbal licorice decoctions can help you feel more refreshed and prepared for your travels.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Licorice root has adaptogenic properties, which means it helps the body adapt to stress and changes in environment, such as those caused by crossing time zones, and promotes a sense of balance and calmness.

Gastroprotective effects: Herbal licorice decoctions can help soothe digestive issues commonly associated with jet lag, such as nausea, bloating, and stomach upset, by protecting the mucous membranes in the gut and reducing inflammation.

Hormone-regulating properties: Licorice root contains compounds that can help regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often exacerbated by travel across time zones, and may help alleviate symptoms like fatigue, insomnia, and mood disturbances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and stress.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes are believed to have calming effects on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root per eight ounces water.
Bring the water to a boil and add the measured root for ten minutes steeping time.
Remove from heat let it cool slightly then strain into a cup using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Add honey or other sweetener to taste if desired and mix well with the decoction.
Drink the entire decoction immediately before bedtime to promote a restful night's sleep.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime decoctions helps with jet lag because of its unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. These compounds have been shown to regulate circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive issues. The decoction's soothing properties can also calm the nervous system, promoting a sense of balance and relaxation. As a result, it can help the body adjust to new time zones more quickly and naturally, making it an effective natural remedy for combating the effects of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Broad-leaved lime decoction's antioxidant properties can help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet lag, which can lead to fatigue, headaches, and mood disturbances.

Adaptogenic properties: The adaptogenic compounds present in broad-leaved lime decoction may help the body adjust to changes in environmental factors, such as time zones, by promoting hormonal balance and reducing stress levels.

Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: The sedative and anti-anxiety effects of broad-leaved lime decoction can help alleviate symptoms like insomnia, restlessness, and anxiety associated with jet lag, allowing for a more comfortable adjustment to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate fatigue and body aches caused by jet lag.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain antispasmodic compounds that can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce stress associated with time changes.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers and two cups of boiling water.
Steep the tilia platyphyllos flowers in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Combine the strained liquid with one cup of cooled water and store it in the refrigerator.
Consume 1/2 cup of the decoction two hours before bedtime and another 1/2 cup upon waking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with jet lag because its calming properties help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by time zone changes. The soothing aroma of lemon balm also promotes relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep in a new environment. Additionally, its mild sedative effects can help alleviate anxiety and irritability that often accompany jet lag. By sipping on a warm cup of lemon balm tea before bedtime, travelers can gently adjust their body's internal clock and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by travel, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag.

Adaptogenic effects: Lemon balm's adaptogenic properties help the body to adjust to new environmental conditions, reducing feelings of fatigue, anxiety, and disorientation that often accompany jet lag.

Calming and sedative effects: The essential oils in lemon balm have a calming and sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and promote relaxation after long-distance travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate circadian rhythms and reduce fatigue.
Buds: Contain a higher concentration of essential oils than leaves or stems, which can provide a more potent and effective dose for addressing jet lag-related stress and insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes in a heat-resistant teapot or mug.
Strain the liquid into a separate container and discard the solids immediately.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to twenty-four hours before consumption.
Drink one cup of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, calmness, and a sense of grounding. The soothing properties of oats can help to ease the stress and anxiety that often accompanies travel across time zones. As our bodies adjust to new rhythms, oats decoctions can provide a gentle and calming influence, helping to regulate our sleep patterns and reduce the symptoms of jet lag. Additionally, oats are rich in fiber and antioxidants, which can also support overall well-being and vitality during periods of transition.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Oat decoctions contain avenanthramides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive discomfort.

Calming and sedative effects: The flavonoids present in oats, particularly avenastrin and avenanthramides, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by time zone changes.

Antioxidant and protective properties: Oat decoctions contain antioxidants such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by jet lag, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate fatigue and discomfort associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried avena sativa herb and four cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herb to infuse its active compounds into the hot water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Consume one cup of the warm avena sativa decoction two to three times daily for optimal effects, ideally with meals.
Prepare each dose in the morning, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes before drinking, and repeat as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, reduce stress, and regulate the body's natural rhythms. The calming properties of beach rose help to ease the discomfort and fatigue that often accompanies time zone changes. By promoting a sense of tranquility and calm, beach rose decoctions can help your body adjust more easily to its new surroundings, reducing the symptoms of jet lag and allowing for a smoother transition into your new environment.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: The decoction helps the body adapt to new time zones by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.

Sedative and calming effects: The beach rose contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting a restful night's sleep and helping to reduce insomnia caused by jet lag.

Antioxidant properties: The decoction's antioxidant activity helps protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel-related inflammation and fatigue, reducing symptoms such as headaches, muscle soreness, and lethargy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Beach rose roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate fatigue and stress associated with jet lag.
Fruits: The fruits of the beach rose plant contain antioxidants that can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rosa x rugosa flowers and leaves for the decoction.
Measure one tablespoon of rosa x rugosa flowers and one teaspoon of dried leaves into a clean glass cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs in the cup and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass cup and discard the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and refrigerate it before drinking within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote natural sleep-wake cycles and calmness. The herbal blend, often combining dates, licorice root, and other soothing ingredients, gently adjusts the body's internal clock to local time zones. As the warm, comforting liquid is sipped, it calms the mind and reduces stress, allowing the body to relax and prepare for a restful night's sleep. With regular use, jet lag symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation begin to subside, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: The combination of herbs in a Chinese date decoction helps the body adapt to changes in environment and time zone, reducing stress and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Sedative and calming effects: The decoction's sedative properties can help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, promoting relaxation and improving the quality of sleep during travel or after arrival at a new destination.

Digestive soothing properties: The herbs in the decoction can help alleviate digestive discomforts such as constipation, diarrhea, or nausea often experienced during air travel, which can further exacerbate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Chinese date plant are believed to help regulate body rhythms and reduce fatigue caused by jet lag, making them a popular choice for herbal remedies.
Roots: The roots of the Chinese date plant contain compounds that can help calm the mind and body, easing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately two tablespoons of dried ziziphus jujuba fruit and place in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with one quart of cold filtered water and stir gently to combine thoroughly.
Store the mixture in the refrigerator for eight hours or overnight allowing for steeping process.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the decoction to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to three days before use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jet lag with herbal decoctions is by consuming a blend of calming herbs like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root. Combine equal parts of each herb in boiling water, then steep for 10-15 minutes before straining and drinking as a tea. This soothing concoction helps regulate sleep patterns, reduces stress and anxiety, and promotes relaxation to ease the symptoms of jet lag.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal decoctions are those caused by disruptions in the body's natural rhythms, such as insomnia, fatigue, and digestive issues. Herbal remedies like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender can help calm the mind and promote relaxation, while others like ashwagandha, ginseng, and ginger can boost energy levels and reduce inflammation.
